what_it_does: Defines a systemd service that launches and supervises a GitHub Actions self-hosted runner by running /home/tim/actions-runner/run.sh, restarting it on failure, and setting PATH and HOME for the process.
observations: Environment explicitly sets PATH and HOME=/home/tim
StandardOutput and StandardError are sent to the journal
standouts: Explicit PATH environment to expose claude, gh, python, etc.
Restart=always to keep the runner persistently available
conventions: Follows standard systemd unit file structure with [Unit], [Service], and [Install]
Uses After= and Wants= network-online.target for network dependency
Uses simple service type and journal logging
concerns: No User= or Group= directive is visible, so the executing identity is unclear from this file alone
Paths are hardcoded to /home/tim, making the unit non-portable
TimeoutStopSec=5min is generous for a simple runner process
notes: This is the systemd glue that keeps a GitHub Actions self-hosted runner process alive and gives it the expected user environment.
questions: Is this intended as a user service or a system service?
How are GitHub runner credentials and tokens supplied?
Which version of the actions-runner is installed at /home/tim/actions-runner?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it runs and supervises a GitHub Actions self-hosted runner.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn the GitHub Actions runner into a reliable, always-on daemon with the right user PATH and home directory.
contribution: Provides the operational service definition that launches, supervises, and restarts the self-hosted runner with an explicit environment.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd service unit that starts and supervises a GitHub Actions self-hosted runner from /home/tim/actions-runner/run.sh with restart, PATH, and HOME configuration.
intention_for_embedding: To keep a GitHub Actions self-hosted runner running as a persistent system service with the user's tooling environment available.
